executive_brief: CourseHub is a course management API providing secure authentication, course lifecycle management, and student enrollment tracking. It implements a role-based access control system (Student/Instructor) using JWT Bearer tokens and manages content distribution based on publication status. The system enforces strict data integrity via enrollment-based deletion guards and automated completion tracking.

architectural_constraints: Course price: strictly in cents, minimum 0
Enrollment progress: values between 0 and 100 inclusive
Course deletion: rejected with 409 Conflict if active enrollments exist
Enrollment: unique constraint on student-course pair
Enrollment: Course must be published to allow enrollment
Course access: Non-owners/Students can only access if published == true
Instructor access: Can only manage, delete, or list courses they own
Student access: Can only access and update their own enrollments

maturity_assessment: The project is in a REFINEMENT state. While the API contracts are comprehensive and the technical mappings are stable, there are critical structural gaps regarding high-level business goals and success metrics. The lack of an explicit scope definition (particularly regarding payment processing and content delivery) creates ambiguity for final implementation.
critical_dependencies: JWT Bearer tokens for Authorization header
Cascading deletion of modules upon successful course deletion
Enrollment strict foreign key dependence on User and Course entities
Automatic trigger: set completed_at timestamp when progress == 100
